rulings.Case Title: SANTIAGO ROBLETO VS. JULIA VARGAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto, Agustin Robleto, Alejandro Robleto And Margarita Lopez'S Motion Under Ccp Sec 1987.1 To Quash Subpoena For Consumer Records, Etc. And For Sanctions Under 1987.2
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 6: Plaintiffs Santiago Robleto's, Agustin Robleto's, Alejandro Robleto's, and Margarita Lopez's Motion Under CCP Sec 1987.1 to Quash Subpoena for Consumer Records, etc. and for Sanctions Under 1987.2 Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MATTHEW FRAZER VS. ROMAN CATHOLIC BISHOP OF SAN JOSE
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-515938, CGC-12-524810 WITH CASE CGC-11-513216
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MATTHEW FRAZER MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-11-515938, CGC-12-524810 WITH CASE CGC-11-513216 The motion to consolidate is denied. The criteria of CCP § 1048(a) are not met. The cases involve three different plaintiffs who allege child sexual abuse allegedly occurring at different times (1992; 1996-2000; and 2011, respectively), in different ways, and at different locations. Jointly trying the cases raises the risk of confusing the jury and prejudicing defendants. Further, Plaintiffs' case authority does not support consolidation in this case. See, e.g., Todd-Stenberg v. Dalkon Shield Claimant Trust (1996) 48 Cal. App. 4th 976 (consolidation of multiple plaintiffs seeking personal injuries from the same medical device) (cited by plaintiffs). In addition, the three cases are in different stages; more than 25 depositions have been taken in the Frazer case and it is set for trial on September 30, 2013. The other two cases are not at issue and discovery is just beginning. Consolidation would delay the Frazer trial. The motion for trial continuance in the Frazer case contingent on the request for consolidation is thus denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY GULISANO VS. JOSEPH CONTE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT JOSEPH CONTE, WATER 2 TABLE, INC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT Defendant Conte's demurrer is overruled. Defendant Water 2 Table, Inc.'s demurrer is sustained, with 10 days leave to amend. Defendants demur to the entire Complaint, and not a specific cause of action. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action against Defendant Conte. Plaintiff's claims are not clearly barred on the face of the First Amended Complaint by the statute of frauds. Plaintiff has not pled sufficient facts to allege a cause of action against Defendant Water 2 Table, Inc. The pleadings are not uncertain. rulings.Case Title: GUILLERMO SIERRA VS. CARPET CONTRACTORS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 14, DEFENDANT CARPET CONTRACTORS, INC., NICHOLAS BOTAITIS, DEMETRI BOTAITIS DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Sustained, with 20 days leave to amend as to the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 6th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th causes of action, overruled as to the 1st, 4th, 10th and 11th causes of action. For the 6th, 7th and 8th causes of action, Plaintiff has not pled compliance with Lab. Code § 2699.3(a). (See Lab. Code § 2699.5) For the 2nd, 3rd, 5th, 6th, 7th, 8th, 9th and 12th causes of action, Mr. Sierra's labor claims are barred by res judicata. See Noble v. Draper (2008) 160 Cal.App.4th 1, 11) ("res judicata bars claims that could have been raised in the first proceeding regardless of whether or not they were raised [citation], collateral estoppel bars only issues that were actually and necessarily decided in the earlier litigation. [ctation.]" Mr. Sierra sought relief for some of his labor claims before the Labor Commission for his employment with Defendants from 1994 to 2012. Mr. Sierra could have raised all of his labor code violation claims from his employment from 1990 to 2012 with the Labor Commission. (See Complaint, 7.) The 1st, 4th and 10th causes of action could not have been raised before the Labor Commission, and res judicata does not apply. (Noble, supra, 160 Cal.App.4th at 12.) The pleadings are not uncertain. The Court takes judicial notice of the Labor Commissioner's Decision that is attached to the opposition. The Court exercises its discretion to hear the demurrer even though it was not timely filed. No default was taken. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/mjm

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 4: Defendants NIKITA GOFMAN'S and SEM GOFMAN'S Motion to Compel Answers to Interrogatories; Request for Production of Documents Pro Tem Judge Philip Andersen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LFG NATIONAL CAPITAL, LLC VS. JOSEPH M. ALIOTO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, August 5, 2013, Line 17, DEFENDANT JOSEPH ALIOTO, THE ALIOTO LAW FIRM, A SOLE PROPRIETORSHIP MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT Denied. Defendants' raises issues of fact that are not the proper subject of a motion to strike. See CCP §436. Further, Defendants have presented extrinsic evidence that is improper. (See Cook Decl., filed Jul. 5, 2013, and Cook Decl., filed Jul. 26, 2013.) Further, Defendants rely on records and evidence without having requested judicial notice. In any event, the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, but "the truth of statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable." (Fremont Indem. Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113.) Defendants' submission of the bill of particulars for the first time in reply is improper and is therefore not considered by the Court. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=302/mjm
